title: cichlanthus chrysanthus
intro: Cichlanthus chrysanthus, also known as the "Golden Trumpet" or "Banj," is a flowering plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Cichlanthus chrysanthus has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its extracts have been found to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, providing potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are typically dried, crushed, or made into teas or infusions, while its extracts are used in various formulations, including capsules, tinctures, and topical creams.
